front: What is CPU utilization as a scheduling criterion?
back: A measure of how busy the CPU is kept. The goal of a scheduler is to maximize CPU utilization, ensuring the processor is active as much as possible.

front: Define throughput in the context of process scheduling.
back: The number of processes that complete their execution per unit time. A scheduler aims to maximize throughput by efficiently moving processes through the system.

The total amount of time taken from when a process arrives in the ready queue to when it completes execution. It is the sum of the time spent waiting in the ready queue, executing on the CPU, and performing I/O operations.
The total amount of time a process spends waiting in the ready queue. A good scheduling algorithm seeks to minimize the waiting time for all processes.

front: What is response time, and why is it crucial for interactive systems?
back: The time from the submission of a request (e.g., a user input) until the first response is produced (not output). It is a key metric for interactive systems as it affects the perceived responsiveness and user experience.
